description: V2 test cases. Create/edit workflows evaluated via image-based LLM judge (DOCX→PNG). Read workflows evaluated via text-based LLM judge (output.txt/output.json).

test_cases.name: List — bullet must use LevelFormat not unicode
test_cases.prompt: Create a Word document with a bulleted list of 5 items: 'Design', 'Develop', 'Test', 'Deploy', 'Monitor'. Use proper Word list formatting.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Document shows a properly indented bulleted list with 5 items (Design, Develop, Test, Deploy, Monitor). Bullet markers are consistent list-style symbols, not ad-hoc unicode characters typed inline.

test_cases.name: Table — dual widths + DXA + ShadingType.CLEAR
test_cases.prompt: Create a Word document with a 3-column table (Name, Department, Salary) with 4 data rows. Add a blue-grey shaded header row. Use US Letter page size.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Document contains a 3-column table with a visually distinct blue-grey shaded header row (Name, Department, Salary) and 4 data rows. Columns are evenly sized. Page is US Letter portrait.

test_cases.name: Heading styles — outlineLevel required for ToC
test_cases.prompt: Create a Word document with 3 sections using Heading 1 for section titles and Heading 2 for subsections. Add a Table of Contents on the first page. Sections: Introduction (2 subs), Methodology (3 subs), Results (2 subs).
test_cases.expected_behavior: A Table of Contents appears on the first page listing Introduction, Methodology, and Results as top-level entries, each with their subsections indented beneath. Headings are visually styled (larger, bold) throughout the document.

test_cases.name: Page break — must be inside Paragraph
test_cases.prompt: Create a 3-page Word document where each page has a distinct section title and body text. Force page breaks between sections.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Document has 3 pages, each beginning with a distinct section title followed by body text. Content does not bleed between sections.

test_cases.name: No \n in paragraphs — separate Paragraph objects
test_cases.prompt: Create a memo Word document with To, From, Date, Subject fields on separate lines, followed by 3 body paragraphs.
test_cases.expected_behavior: The memo shows To, From, Date, and Subject each on their own line at the top. Below, 3 clearly separated body paragraphs appear. No fields are merged into a single block of text.

test_cases.name: Footer with tab stops — not table
test_cases.prompt: Create a Word document with a footer containing: company name on the left, document title centered, page number on the right.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Footer shows three elements in a single line: company name aligned left, a title text centered, and a page number on the right. Layout is clean without table borders in the footer.

test_cases.name: ImageRun — type parameter required
test_cases.prompt: Create a Word document with a placeholder company logo image (generate a simple 100x50px colored rectangle as PNG) and a title 'Annual Report 2026'. Image should appear at the top.
test_cases.expected_behavior: A colored rectangle image appears at the top of the document, followed by the heading 'Annual Report 2026'. The image is visible and not broken/missing.

test_cases.name: Footnotes — proper docx-js API
test_cases.prompt: Create a research summary Word document with 3 paragraphs. Add footnotes: paragraph 1 cites 'Smith et al. 2024', paragraph 2 cites 'See appendix A', paragraph 3 cites 'Data from WHO 2025'.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Each paragraph has a visible superscript footnote number. At the bottom of the page, footnotes show the three citations: Smith et al. 2024, See appendix A, and Data from WHO 2025.

test_cases.name: US Letter page size explicit — not A4 default
test_cases.prompt: Create a US Letter format business letter (8.5" x 11") with 1-inch margins. Include a letterhead, date, recipient address, 2-paragraph body, and signature block.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Document appears as a portrait business letter with letterhead at the top, a date line, recipient address block, two body paragraphs, and a signature block at the bottom. Margins appear approximately 1 inch on all sides.

test_cases.name: Numbered list — independent vs continuing references
test_cases.prompt: Create a Word document with two separate numbered lists. List 1: Steps to install (3 items). List 2: Steps to configure (4 items). Each list must start numbering from 1 independently.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Two numbered lists appear. The first list (install steps) is numbered 1, 2, 3. The second list (configure steps) restarts at 1, 2, 3, 4 — it does NOT continue from 4.

test_cases.name: Extract all text with structure preserved
test_cases.prompt: Read the attached Word document and extract all its text content preserving the heading hierarchy. Save the result to output.txt using # for Heading 1, ## for Heading 2, and plain text for paragraphs.
test_cases.expected_behavior: output.txt contains the extracted document text. Heading levels are marked with # and ## prefixes. Paragraph text is present. Content is not empty.

test_cases.name: Extract table data as structured output
test_cases.prompt: Read the attached Word document and extract the table data as a JSON array of objects using the header row as keys. Save the result to output.json.
test_cases.expected_behavior: output.json is valid JSON containing an array of objects. Each object has keys matching the table's header row. The array has one entry per data row with non-empty values.

test_cases.name: Summarize document — read then write
test_cases.prompt: Read the attached Word document and create a new 1-page executive summary Word document based on its content.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Output is a concise 1-page .docx executive summary. It contains key points from the fixture document written in clear prose. Layout is clean and readable.

test_cases.name: Read and compute statistics from table
test_cases.prompt: Read the attached Word document, extract the numeric data from the table, and save a report to output.txt with: sum of each column, average of each column, and which row has the highest total.
test_cases.expected_behavior: output.txt contains a statistics report. It shows column sums, column averages, and identifies the highest-total row by name or index. Values are clearly labeled.

test_cases.name: Find and replace — unpack→edit→pack workflow
test_cases.prompt: Open the attached Word document and replace all occurrences of 'Company' with 'Acme Corp'. Preserve all formatting.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Every instance of 'Company' in the document has been replaced with 'Acme Corp'. All original formatting (fonts, bold, tables, etc.) is preserved.

test_cases.name: Add tracked changes (w:ins / w:del)
test_cases.prompt: Open the attached contract Word document and use tracked changes to: (1) change '30 days' to '60 days' in the Payment Terms section, (2) delete the sentence containing 'Party A reserves the right'.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Document shows '60 days' where '30 days' appeared in the Payment Terms section. The sentence containing 'Party A reserves the right' is absent from the document.

test_cases.name: Change text formatting — bold, italic, underline
test_cases.prompt: Open the attached document. Find all instances of the word 'important' and make them bold. Find all instances of 'Note:' and make them italic and underline.
test_cases.expected_behavior: All occurrences of 'important' appear bold in the document. All occurrences of 'Note:' appear italic and underlined. No other text has been reformatted.

test_cases.name: Insert header and footer
test_cases.prompt: Open the attached document. Add a header with the text 'CONFIDENTIAL' centered, and a footer with page numbers centered.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Every page shows 'CONFIDENTIAL' centered in the header. A centered page number appears in the footer. The original document content is unchanged.

test_cases.name: Merge/combine multiple documents
test_cases.prompt: You have two Word documents available: simple_report.docx and newsletter_raw.docx. Merge them into a single document with simple_report.docx content first, then newsletter_raw.docx content on a new page.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Output is a single .docx with two distinct content sections separated by a page break. Both sections contain readable text content.

test_cases.name: Convert .doc to .docx
test_cases.prompt: Convert the attached legacy .doc file to .docx format. Preserve all formatting, tables, and content.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Output is a valid .docx file with readable content. Text, headings, and any tables are visible and appear uncorrupted.

test_cases.name: Convert .docx to Markdown and save as report
test_cases.prompt: Convert the attached Word document to Markdown format. Save the Markdown output into a new Word document (.docx) as a code block or preformatted section, so the Markdown syntax is visible.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Output .docx contains the Markdown representation of the original document. Headings appear as # and ## syntax, tables as ASCII/pipe-delimited format, bold/italic as * markers. All content from the fixture is present.

test_cases.name: Empty document — valid but minimal
test_cases.prompt: Create an empty Word document with just default formatting. File should be valid and openable.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Output is a valid .docx that opens without errors. It shows a blank page with default margins and no content errors.

test_cases.name: Multi-page document — page breaks and repetition
test_cases.prompt: Create a Word document with 10 pages of Lorem Ipsum text. Include a page break between every 2 pages.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Document contains multiple pages of Lorem Ipsum text. Page breaks are visible at regular intervals. Document opens without errors and text is readable.

test_cases.name: Special characters — Unicode and symbols
test_cases.prompt: Create a Word document containing text with various special characters: emoji (😀🎉), math symbols (∑∫√), currency (€¥₹), and other Unicode: 你好 (Chinese), مرحبا (Arabic).
test_cases.expected_behavior: All special characters render correctly and are readable: emoji, math symbols, currency signs, Chinese characters, and Arabic text all appear without garbled or replacement characters.

test_cases.name: Mixed content — text, images, tables, all together
test_cases.prompt: Create a Word document with: title heading, intro paragraph, a 2x2 table with mixed content (text and numbers), an image below the table, conclusion text, and footer with page number.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Document shows all elements in order: a title heading, intro paragraph, a 2x2 table, an image, conclusion text, and a footer with a page number. Layout is clean with no overlapping elements.

test_cases.name: Tracked deletions — read and preserve
test_cases.prompt: Read the attached Word document that contains tracked deletions. Create a new Word document (.docx) summarizing: (1) what text was deleted, (2) who made the changes, (3) whether the deletions are still visible (accepted or pending).
test_cases.expected_behavior: Output .docx contains a readable summary report with three clearly labeled sections covering: the deleted text content, author names of the changes, and the acceptance status of the deletions.
